title: Surveillance of rotavirus - Canada.ca
content:
## Surveillance of rotavirus
Learn about rotavirus  surveillance.
- Number of cases of rotavirus in Canada
- Number of cases of rotavirus around the world
- For more information

## Number of cases  of rotavirus in Canada
In  Canada, rotavirus is a common cause of gastroenteritis in children.
It has been associated with considerable health care resource utilization.  Approximately 7,500 cases of rotavirus illness are hospitalized annually.  Most of these hospitalizations are young children (younger than 5 years old).

## Number of cases of rotavirus around the world
Rotavirus is the leading cause of severe diarrhea  (gastroenteritis) in children throughout the world. About 95% of children contract the infection by 5 years of age.
Mortality due to rotavirus infection is much  higher in developing countries. The World Health Organization estimates that  453,000 children aged less than 5 years died in 2008 from vaccine-preventable rotavirus infections. Globally, these rotavirus deaths account for approximately 5% of all child deaths.
Disease outbreaks demonstrate a seasonal pattern in temperate climates, where the disease is more pronounced during  drier and cooler months.

## For more information
- World Health Organization: Estimated rotavirus deaths for children under 5 years of age (2008)
- Epidemiology and Infection: “Estimated Hospitalizations Attributed to Norovirus and Rotavirus Infection in Canada, 2006 to 2010”
